title: Dryopteris stierii
taxonomy: Dryopteris stierii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which comprises about 250 species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is a member of the order Polypodiales, which includes over 11,000 species of ferns. Dryopteris stierii is native to Central America, specifically in Costa Rica. It is a relatively rare species, and its classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Dryopteris stierii, also known as Stier's wood fern, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris stierii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Stier's wood fern include teas and infusions, as well as extracts and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
